shadow_event.query: 打开新窗口后 Codex Context Monitor 会自动更新到新窗口么？

transcript.content: 需要做成，我点击状态栏工具，直接能够在显示的方法； 并且能够持续展示变化；而不需要我经常刷新； 可以打开一个编辑器来展示这些内容，也可以在terminal里展示，甚至是tail的方式，但是要自动； 而不是我要手动输入什么指令；
我已经把“可视化 context 大小”这层先做出来了，仓库侧现在有两个可直接接入的展示面： 1. 固定面板文件 路径： [live-panel.md](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/reports/generated/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc/live-panel.md)
已经接上了。 我改了两层：
为什么 和 Project Assistant 有关系？
因为我刚才找到并能直接修改的“状态栏宿主代码”，不是 OpenAI 官方 Codex 扩展本体，而是你本机装的这个扩展： - `~/.codex/skills/project-assistant/integrations/vscode-host/extension.js`
单独做，不要碰assitant 要有边界；
单独扩展已经做了，而且 `Project Assistant` 那边的误接线我已经撤回了。 现在机器上多了一个独立 VS Code 扩展：
这两个做重了吧，应该合并成1个； 先简短展示，点击后，出一个小窗口，在展示详情； 可以自己动态更新的； live pannel.md可以去除掉了；
已经按你说的收成一条了： - `Project Assistant` 的接线我撤回了 - 改成一个独立扩展 `Codex Context Monitor` - 状态栏现在应该只做 `简短展示` - 点击后打开 `小窗口详情` - 详情窗口会跟着后台轮询自动更新 - `live-panel.md` 已经删掉，不再作为主入口
打开新窗口后 Codex Context Monitor 会自动更新到新窗口么？
会，但有边界。 按现在这个 standalone 扩展的实现：

decision.archive_summary: The conversation moved from a general request for automatically visible, continuously updating context display to a narrowed implementation: a standalone VS Code extension rather than any Project Assistant integration. An intermediate detour about Project Assistant ownership was resolved when the user required a strict boundary and the assistant said that integration was removed. The active design was then consolidated into one surface: brief status in the status bar, click to open a small detail window, auto-refreshing, and removal of live-panel.md as a primary UI.
decision.reasoning_summary: This is the same active topic, so relation=continue. The latest user question about whether Codex Context Monitor auto-updates in a new window depends on the recent implementation context that it is now a standalone extension with status bar summary and auto-updating detail view, so those recent raw turns should remain. Older turns about the earlier display ideas and the resolved Project Assistant detour are no longer needed as raw prompt material. The user’s boundary rule from t7 is durable and should survive as a semantic pin even if its raw turn leaves the prompt.
